root_text.iti92:2.1: “Saṅghāṭikaṇṇe cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gahetvā piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandho assa pāde pādaṁ nikkhipanto,
root_text.iti92:2.2: so ca hoti abhijjhālu kāmesu tibbasārāgo byāpannacitto paduṭṭhamanasaṅkappo muṭṭhassati asampajāno asamāhito vibbhantacitto pākatindriyo;
root_text.iti92:2.3: atha kho so ārakāva mayhaṁ, ahañca tassa.
root_text.iti92:2.5: Dhammañhi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na passati.
root_text.iti92:3.1: Yojanasate cepi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vihareyya.
root_text.iti92:3.2: So ca hoti anabhijjhālu kāmesu na tibbasārāgo abyāpannacitto apaduṭṭhamanasaṅkappo upaṭṭhitassati sampajāno samāhito ekaggacitto saṁvutindriyo;
root_text.iti92:3.3: atha kho so santikeva mayhaṁ, ahañca tassa.
root_text.iti92:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti92:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti92:2.1: “Mendicants, suppose a mendicant were to hold the corner of my cloak and follow behind me step by step.
translation_text.iti92:2.2: Yet they covet sensual pleasures; they’re infatuated, full of ill will and malicious intent. They are unmindful, lacking situational awareness and immersion, with straying minds and undisciplined faculties.
translation_text.iti92:2.3: Then they are far from me, and I from them.
translation_text.iti92:2.5: Because that mendicant does not see the teaching.
translation_text.iti92:2.6: Not seeing the teaching, they do not see me.
translation_text.iti92:3.1: Suppose a mendicant were to live a hundred leagues away.
translation_text.iti92:3.2: Yet they do not covet sensual pleasures; they’re not infatuated, or full of ill will and malicious intent. They have established mindfulness, situational awareness and immersion, with unified mind and restrained faculties.
translation_text.iti92:3.3: Then they are close to me, and I to them.
translation_text.iti92:3.5: Because that mendicant sees the teaching.
translation_text.iti92:3.6: Seeing the teaching, they see me.”
translation_text.iti92:4.3: yet see how distant they are—
translation_text.iti92:5.1: An astute person who has understood
translation_text.iti92:5.4: as a lake free of wind.
translation_text.iti92:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti92:2.6: na maṁ passati → maṁ na passati (sya-all)

reference_text.iti92:5.1: csp1ed18.257, csp2ed18.257, ms18It_657, ndp20.244, pts-vp-pli92, vns206
